annotations.label: Standard deviation (informal)

anchor.snippet: the standard deviation is a measure of the amount of variation of the values of a variable about its (arithmetic) average

annotations.note: Mathlib defines variance for real-valued random variables but has no standard-deviation declaration named as such.

annotations.label: Standard deviation as square root of variance

anchor.snippet: is the square root of its variance

annotations.note: No `stdDev`/`standardDeviation` declaration exists in Mathlib; only `ProbabilityTheory.variance` is defined (loogle for `"stdDev"` returned zero hits).

annotations.label: Sample standard deviation (terminology)

anchor.snippet: the term standard deviation of the sample or sample standard deviation

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of sample standard deviation.

annotations.label: Standard error of the sample mean

anchor.section: Relationship with standard error and statistical significance
anchor.snippet: The sample mean's standard error is the standard deviation of the set of means

annotations.note: No definition of standard error of the sample mean was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Population SD of grades of eight students

anchor.section: Population standard deviation of grades of eight students
anchor.snippet: Suppose that the entire population of interest is eight students in a particular class

annotations.note: This numerical illustration is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bessel's correction

anchor.section: Population standard deviation of grades of eight students
anchor.snippet: This is known as Bessel's correction

annotations.note: No declaration referring to Bessel's correction or the (n-1) divisor was found.

annotations.label: SD of average height for adult men

anchor.section: Standard deviation of average height for adult men
anchor.snippet: the average height for adult men in the United States is about 69 inches

annotations.note: Real-world numerical example not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Standard deviation of a random variable

anchor.snippet: Let μ be the expected value (the average) of a random variable X with probability density function f

annotations.note: Variance of a random variable is defined as `Var[X] = 𝔼[(X − 𝔼[X])²]`, but its square root (the SD) is not separately named.

annotations.label: SD of a probability distribution

anchor.snippet: The standard deviation of a probability distribution is the same as that of a random variable having that distribution

annotations.note: Mathlib's `variance` is parameterised by the underlying measure, but there is no dedicated SD-of-distribution definition.

annotations.label: Existence of standard deviation

anchor.snippet: Not all random variables have a standard deviation

annotations.note: Mathlib characterises finiteness of `evariance` via `MemLp X 2`, the analogue of variance/SD existence.

annotations.label: Cauchy distribution has no SD

anchor.snippet: The Cauchy distribution has neither a mean nor a standard deviation

annotations.note: Mathlib has no Cauchy distribution definition (loogle for `cauchy`+`Distribution` returned zero hits) and no statement about non-existence of its moments.

annotations.label: SD of a discrete random variable (equiprobable)

anchor.snippet: In the case where X takes random values from a finite data set

annotations.note: Variance specialises to the discrete equiprobable case via a uniform measure, but no dedicated formula is provided.

annotations.label: SD of a discrete random variable (weighted)

anchor.snippet: If, instead of having equal probabilities, the values have different probabilities

annotations.note: Captured implicitly by `variance` on a discrete weighted measure but no explicit weighted-sum formula.

annotations.label: SD of a continuous random variable

anchor.snippet: The standard deviation of a continuous real-valued random variable X with probability density function

annotations.note: Variance is expressed as an integral form `∫ (X − 𝔼[X])² dμ`, generalising the continuous-density formula.

annotations.label: SD of the log-normal distribution

anchor.snippet: in the case of the log-normal distribution with parameters

annotations.note: Log-normal distribution is not defined in Mathlib (loogle for `logNormal` returned zero hits).

annotations.label: Sample standard deviation estimator

anchor.snippet: Such a statistic is called an estimator

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formal notion of statistical estimator for SD.

annotations.label: Uncorrected sample standard deviation

anchor.snippet: This estimator, denoted by s N , is known as the uncorrected sample standard deviation

annotations.note: No declaration corresponding to s_N exists.

annotations.label: Consistency of uncorrected sample SD

anchor.snippet: This is a consistent estimator

annotations.note: The strong law of large numbers is in Mathlib but consistency of the SD estimator is not stated.

annotations.label: Corrected sample standard deviation

anchor.snippet: yielding the corrected sample standard deviation, denoted by s

annotations.note: No declaration for the Bessel-corrected estimator s.

annotations.label: Unbiasedness of s² with Bessel's correction

anchor.snippet: An unbiased estimator for the variance is given by applying Bessel's correction

annotations.note: No unbiased-estimator statement involving the (n-1) divisor is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jensen's inequality forces downward bias in s

anchor.snippet: Here taking the square root introduces further downward bias, by Jensen's inequality

annotations.note: Jensen's inequality for concave functions is formalized as `ConcaveOn.le_map_integral`; the specific application to sqrt of sample variance is not.

annotations.label: Unbiased sample SD via correction factor

anchor.snippet: For the normal distribution, an unbiased estimator is given by

annotations.note: Correction factor c_4(n) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sampling distribution is scaled chi

anchor.snippet: the sampling distribution of the sample standard deviation follows a (scaled) chi distribution

annotations.note: The chi and chi-square distributions are not defined in Mathlib (loogle for `chiSquared` returned zero hits).

annotations.label: Approximation replacing N−1 with N−1.5

anchor.snippet: An approximation can be given by replacing N − 1 with N − 1.5

annotations.note: Numerical approximation not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Confidence interval for sampled SD

anchor.section: Confidence interval of a sampled standard deviation
anchor.snippet: where [MATH] is the p -th quantile of the chi-square distribution

annotations.note: No confidence-interval construction for sample SD is formalized.
annotations.moderation_flag: Anchor snippet contains '[MATH]' literal; ideally should be replaced with text actually present in extracted prose (kept verbatim to honor the preserve-id rule).

annotations.label: Range bound on standard deviation

anchor.snippet: an upper bound on the standard deviation s is given by

annotations.note: Popoviciu's inequality `variance_le_sq_of_bounded` bounds variance by `((b-a)/2)^2` for bounded RVs, the variance analogue of the sample-range SD bound.

annotations.label: Range rule heuristic

anchor.snippet: This so-called range rule is useful in sample size estimation

annotations.note: Heuristic rule for sample size is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Location invariance and scaling of SD

anchor.snippet: The standard deviation is invariant under changes in location , and scales directly with the scale of the random variable

annotations.note: `variance_add_const` and `variance_const_mul` give the variance-level analogues; the SD-level statement is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: SD of a sum of two random variables

anchor.snippet: The standard deviation of the sum of two random variables can be related to their individual standard deviations and the covariance between them

annotations.note: `variance_add` proves `Var(X+Y) = Var X + Var Y + 2 Cov(X,Y)`, the variance form of the stated SD identity.

annotations.label: Computational formula for the variance

anchor.snippet: the standard deviation is equal to the square root of the difference between the average of the squares of the values and the square of the average value

annotations.note: `variance_eq_sub` states `Var[X] = 𝔼[X²] − (𝔼[X])²` under a probability measure.

annotations.label: Three populations with same mean, different SDs

anchor.snippet: each of the three populations {0, 0, 14, 14}, {0, 6, 8, 14} and {6, 6, 8, 8} has a mean of 7

annotations.note: Pedagogical example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mean absolute deviation (alternative)

anchor.snippet: An example is the mean absolute deviation , which might be considered a more direct measure of average distance

annotations.note: No `meanAbsoluteDeviation` declaration is present in Mathlib (loogle returned zero hits).

annotations.label: 5-sigma in particle physics

anchor.snippet: Particle physics conventionally uses a standard of

annotations.note: Application-domain convention not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weather: coastal vs inland SD

anchor.snippet: the range of daily maximum temperatures for cities near the coast is smaller than for cities inland

annotations.note: Application example not formalized.

annotations.label: Finance: Stock A vs Stock B

anchor.snippet: assume an investor had to choose between two stocks

annotations.note: Finance example not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geometric interpretation of SD

anchor.snippet: the distance between P and M (which is the same as the orthogonal distance between P and the line L )

annotations.note: No geometric formulation of SD as distance to the diagonal is stated.

annotations.label: Chebyshev's inequality

anchor.snippet: Chebyshev's inequality ensures that, for all distributions for which the standard deviation is defined

annotations.note: Chebyshev's inequality is proved as `meas_ge_le_variance_div_sq` (plus an `evariance` variant).

annotations.label: Central limit theorem

anchor.snippet: The central limit theorem states that the distribution of an average of many independent, identically distributed random variables tends toward the famous bell-shaped normal distribution

annotations.note: No central limit theorem declaration was found in Mathlib (loogle for `centralLimit` returned zero hits).

annotations.label: Proportion within z standard deviations

anchor.snippet: the proportion of data values within z standard deviations of the mean is defined by

annotations.note: Normal-CDF based proportion formula is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 68–95–99.7 rule (empirical rule)

anchor.snippet: This is known as the 68–95–99.7 rule , or the empirical rule

annotations.note: Empirical rule percentages are not formalized.

annotations.label: Standard deviation matrix

anchor.snippet: It is the symmetric square root of the covariance matrix

annotations.note: No SD-matrix (symmetric square root of covariance matrix) declaration was found.

annotations.label: Eigenstructure gives error ellipsoid axes

anchor.snippet: correspond to the axes of the 1 sd error ellipsoid of the multivariate normal distribution

annotations.note: Error-ellipsoid characterisation is not present.

annotations.label: SD of projection onto unit vector

anchor.snippet: The standard deviation of the projection of the multivariate distribution

annotations.note: No statement about SD of one-dimensional projection of a multivariate distribution.

annotations.label: SD of conditional slice

anchor.snippet: The standard deviation of a slice of the multivariate distribution

annotations.note: No conditional-slice SD statement was found.

annotations.label: Mahalanobis whitening transform

anchor.snippet: This is called the Mahalanobis whitening transform

annotations.note: Mahalanobis/whitening transform not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mean minimizes mean squared deviation

anchor.section: Relationship between standard deviation and mean
anchor.snippet: it is possible to show that σ ( r ) has a unique minimum at the mean

annotations.note: The variational characterisation of the mean as minimiser of E[(X−r)²] is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Coefficient of variation

anchor.section: Relationship between standard deviation and mean
anchor.snippet: Variability can also be measured by the coefficient of variation

annotations.note: No `coefficientOfVariation` definition exists in Mathlib (loogle returned zero hits).

annotations.label: Standard deviation of the mean (SDOM)

anchor.snippet: the standard deviation of the mean ( SDOM ) is related to the standard deviation of the distribution by

annotations.note: `IndepFun.variance_sum` gives the variance of an independent sum but the σ/√N SDOM formula is not stated.

annotations.label: Running standard deviation formula

anchor.snippet: The following two formulas can represent a running (repeatedly updated) standard deviation

annotations.note: Welford-style online update formulas are not formalized.

annotations.label: Standard deviation index (SDI)

anchor.snippet: The standard deviation index (SDI) is used in external quality assessments

annotations.note: Quality-control SDI metric is not formalized.

annotations.label: Average absolute deviation (alternative to SD)

anchor.snippet: Standard deviation is algebraically simpler

annotations.note: The average/mean absolute deviation is not defined in Mathlib (loogle returned zero hits).
